summary: Frontend-only Home responsive validation completed on http://127.0.0.1:3456. Requested FAQ/CTA additions and 1366px/1440px balancing checks passed visually and functionally, but console shows a React hydration runtime error (#418) that needs fixing.

react_native_issues.issue: Console logs PAGE ERROR: Minified React error #418 on Home load, indicating hydration mismatch on web preview.

action_items: Fix React hydration mismatch on Home web render (likely desktop mode/layout branch divergence between initial render and mounted state).
Re-run responsive regression after hydration fix to ensure no visual/layout jump on 1366px and 1440px.
Keep FAQ and CTA sections as implemented; both are present and readable in current build.
critical_code_review_comments: HomeScreen computes desktop mode via post-mount window resize state (`isDesktop`) while also using width-derived layout values, which can produce SSR/static markup mismatch and hydration error #418.
Using divergent render branches before/after mount for responsive logic should be stabilized to avoid hydration fallback on web.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Scope tested: Home responsiveness at 1366px and 1440px, FAQ/CTA visibility, CTA navigation, mobile home load, and gallery/partner/vendor regression checks. n8n availability and booking submission webhooks are MOCKED and not validated in this run.
rca_of_issue: Reproduction: open Home at http://127.0.0.1:3456 and inspect console; error appears as Minified React #418. Likely cause is hydration mismatch from responsive branching (`isDesktop` updated after mount with window.innerWidth) producing different initial vs hydrated trees. Mitigation: align initial responsive state with client width safely or gate width-dependent branch until mounted to keep first render deterministic.
